name: Saint-Vincent and English Falls Trail
name_original: Cascade des Anglais et Cascade Saint Vincent – randonnée depuis Vernet-les-Bains – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
description: Hike the easy 2.4-mile Saint-Vincent and English Falls Trail in the Pyrénées Catalanes, featuring two beautiful waterfalls.
region_intro: Hiking around Vernet-Les-Bains offers diverse terrain at the foot of the Canigou Massif, the easternmost high summit of the Pyrenees. The region is characterized by the verdant Cady Valley, featuring forests of Laricio pines, beeches, and chestnut trees. Numerous waterfalls, including the Saint-Vincent and English Falls, are accessible via various trails, providing natural features for hikers. Best hiking trails around Vernet-Les-Bains The most popular hiking route is Saint-Vincent and English Falls Trail, an easy 2.4 miles (3.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This path leads to two notable waterfalls in the Cady Valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is English Falls – Saint-Vincent Waterfall loop from Vernet-les-Bains, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This loop offers views of both waterfalls and the surrounding forested valley. Local hikers also love the Saint-Martin du Canigou Abbey and Goa Tower Loop, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 5 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Vernet-Les-Bains is defined by mountain trails, forested valleys, and waterfall routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging ascents with considerable elevation gain. The routes in Vernet-Les-Bains are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 550 reviews. More than 3500 hikers have used komoot to explore Vernet-Les-Bains's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Vernet-Les-Bains?
A: Vernet-Les-Bains offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the verdant Cady Valley, lead to stunning waterfalls, and ascend towards the majestic Canigou Massif.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Vernet-Les-Bains?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958773439/saint-vincent-and-english-falls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Vincent and English Falls Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.4-mile (3.8 km) path that leads to two beautiful waterfalls and is generally completed in under 1.5 hours.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Vernet-Les-Bains?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its enchanting waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958773439/saint-vincent-and-english-falls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Vincent and English Falls Trail&lt;/a&gt; for a direct experience, or opt for a longer loop like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26040941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;English Falls – Saint-Vincent Waterfall loop from Vernet-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt; to enjoy both cascades and the surrounding forested valley.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958848239/saint-martin-du-canigou-abbey-and-goa-tower-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Martin du Canigou Abbey and Goa Tower Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.2-mile (14.8 km) trail that takes you through varied terrain and offers panoramic views from the Goa Tower.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hiking around Vernet-Les-Bains means immersing yourself in the natural beauty of the Cady Valley and the Canigou Massif. You'll encounter lush forests of Laricio pines, beeches, and chestnut trees, the flowing Cady River, and several picturesque waterfalls. Many trails also offer breathtaking panoramic views of the mountains and valleys.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, several trails incorporate significant historical and architectural sites. A notable example is the Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958848239/saint-martin-du-canigou-abbey-and-goa-tower-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbey of Saint-Martin du Canigou&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also reveal military remains like Vauban's cistern and redoubts, or former ore quarries, adding a historical dimension to your hike.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Vernet-Les-Bains?
A: The trails in Vernet-Les-Bains are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 550 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the stunning waterfalls, and the rewarding panoramic views, especially from points like the Goa Tower.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking trails passing through or near Vernet-Les-Bains?
A: Yes, the region is part of the renowned GR10, a long-distance hiking trail that traverses the Pyrenees from the Atlantic to the Mediterranean. Other long-distance routes like the HexaTrek also have stages in the Eastern Pyrenees near Vernet-les-Bains, offering extensive trekking opportunities.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Vernet-Les-Bains?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer is also popular, especially for higher altitude routes, though it can be warmer. Winter hiking is possible, particularly on lower trails, but requires appropriate gear and awareness of snow conditions in the higher elevations of the Canigou Massif.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Vernet-Les-Bains offers several excellent circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26040941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;English Falls – Saint-Vincent Waterfall loop from Vernet-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958848239/saint-martin-du-canigou-abbey-and-goa-tower-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Martin du Canigou Abbey and Goa Tower Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides varied terrain and stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or panoramic spots worth seeking out?
A: Many trails offer incredible panoramic views. The Tour de Goa, for instance, provides an exceptional 360° vista of the surrounding mountains and valleys, often featuring an orientation table to help identify peaks. Higher points on the Canigou Massif also offer views towards the Roussillon plain and the Mediterranean Sea on clear days.

Q: What is the highest point I can reach on a hike from Vernet-Les-Bains?
A: Vernet-Les-Bains is nestled at the foot of the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canigou Massif&lt;/a&gt;, which peaks at 2,785 meters. While direct routes from the village to the summit are challenging and for experienced hikers, many trails offer ascents towards its slopes, providing access to stunning high-altitude scenery and views.
tips: This waterfall, named after the Saint Vincent River, offers a lovely pool to cool off after the sweaty climb. But you can also continue for ten minutes to the Cascade des Anglais, which is also very beautiful.

A very pleasant route in summer thanks to the coolness and the constant presence of the Saint-Vincent River, which flows down from the Conques du Canigó. The atmosphere of these gorges and the roar of the waterfalls will captivate you. Be careful, however, as this steep trail carries risks, including rockfalls.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Saint-Vincent and English Falls Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is designed as an easy hike, and most people complete it in approximately 1 hour and 25 minutes. This duration allows for enjoying the scenery and the waterfalls at a comfortable pace.

faqs.question: Is the Saint-Vincent and English Falls Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is rated as 'easy', making it suitable for beginners and families. Its relatively short distance and manageable elevation gain provide an enjoyable outdoor experience without excessive challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain typical of an easy hike in a natural park, likely consisting of well-maintained paths, some gentle inclines, and sections near the waterfalls. Given its 'easy' rating, you won't encounter overly steep or technical sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions on this route are two beautiful waterfalls: the Saint-Vincent Waterfall and the English Falls. These natural features offer picturesque spots for rest and photography.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead located and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near Vernet-les-Bains, within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes. Specific parking information isn't detailed, but generally, trails in regional parks have designated parking areas nearby. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Saint-Vincent and English Falls Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Most trails within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes allow dogs, provided they are kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or permits required for day hikes on public trails within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes. However, always respect park regulations, which are usually posted at trailheads.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during spring, summer, and early autumn. During these seasons, the weather is usually pleasant, and the waterfalls are often at their most impressive. Winter hiking might be possible but could involve colder temperatures and potentially slippery conditions.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this easy hike, comfortable walking shoes or light hiking boots are recommended. Bring water, snacks, sun protection, and a light jacket, as weather can change. A camera is also a good idea for capturing the waterfalls.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located near Vernet-les-Bains, you can expect to find amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the village. It's a good idea to plan any stops for food or supplies before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Hiking in the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes, you can expect to see diverse flora typical of the Pyrenean foothills. While specific wildlife sightings are never guaranteed, the area is home to various bird species and small mammals. Always observe from a distance and do not disturb the natural habitat.
